claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I need to ask you a few questions.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex and he's 8 years old. I'd like to know if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to ask you a few questions:

First, do you currently live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Great! Now, does Alex live with you in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect! That's helpful to know.

Now I need to understand your immigration status. Are you a UK citizen, or do you have settled status, pre-settled status under the EU Settlement Scheme, or another immigration status?

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent! Just to confirm a few final details:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex (for example, Alex's other parent or another family member)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- **Age requirement**: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he qualifies
- **Residency**: You're a UK citizen living in the UK, which meets the residency requirement
- **Responsibility**: Alex lives with you, which means you're responsible for him
- **No duplicate claims**: No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex (only one person can claim per child)

You meet all the eligibility criteria and can proceed with claiming Child Benefit for Alex. 

**Please note**: If either you or your partner has an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. You can still claim Child Benefit, but you might have to pay back some or all of it through your tax return.

Would you like information on how to make your Child Benefit claim?

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old, which is under 16, so he meets the age requirement for Child Benefit. The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK, which satisfies the residency requirement. Alex lives with the claimant, which means the claimant is responsible for Alex under the rules (as they live together). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which is required since only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 8-year-old son Alex. Since Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, the claimant meets all the eligibility requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

Alex lives with the claimant, which means the claimant is responsible for Alex under the rules (as they live together). No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, which is required since only one person can claim Child Benefit for each child. All eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is a UK citizen living in the UK with their 8-year-old son Alex. Since Alex is under 16, lives with the claimant, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him, the claimant meets all the eligibility requirements to claim Child Benefit for Alex.
